subject: response regulator

facts.label: smeRS
facts.desc: smeRS is a two component regulatory system for smeABC where smeR is a response regulator, while smeS is a sensor kinase.

facts.label: protein of two-component regulatory system modulating antibiotic efflux
facts.desc: A protein, either a histidine kinase or a response regulator, that is part of a two-component regulatory system that directly or indirectly change rates of antibiotic efflux.

facts.label: baeSR
facts.desc: BaeSR is a two component regulatory system for efflux proteins in Gram-negative bacteria. BaeR is a response regulator, while BaeS is a sensor kinase.

facts.label: vanS
facts.desc: VanS is similar to histidine protein kinases like EnvZ and acts as a response regulator by activating VanR. VanS is required for high level transcription of other van glycopeptide resistance genes.

facts.label: phosphoethanolamine transferase conferring colistin resistance
facts.desc: This group of enzymes catalyzes the addition of a phosphoethanolamine group to another molecule. The addition of this moiety to lipid A in bacterial species is often associated with polymyxin (otherwise known as colistin) resistance.
